agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, while Operations demands location never be shown to protect driver retention.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver employed or contracted by the company to fulfill last-mile delivery of parcels to customers."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Active delivery","definition":"The period between a courier picking up a parcel and completing its delivery to the customer."},{"term":"Customer","definition":"The end recipient of a parcel who has placed an order and is awaiting delivery."},{"term":"Courier visibility","definition":"The degree to which a courier's location or progress is exposed to external parties such as customers."},{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — a computed prediction of when a courier will reach the delivery address."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"During an active delivery, the customer must receive some form of delivery progress information.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The form and granularity of progress information is a business policy decision that leadership can change. The need to provide some tracking is a competitive requirement, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an active delivery, the customer can access a tracking view that provides delivery progress information."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Courier personally identifiable location data must not be exposed in a way that compromises driver safety or creates a surveillance experience.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Driver safety and privacy are fundamental obligations. Exposing exact GPS coordinates creates real physical safety risks (stalking, confrontation) and potential regulatory liability. This is a hard constraint regardless of business policy.","testable_assertion":"The system never exposes raw GPS latitude/longitude coordinates of a courier to a customer."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivery tracking information must be available only during the active delivery window for the specific customer's order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Showing courier location outside of an active delivery or to unrelated parties would be a privacy violation and a data integrity breach.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery that is not in 'active' status, the tracking endpoint returns no courier location or progress data."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The tracking experience must provide sufficient transparency to reduce 'where is my order' support contacts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The threshold of 'sufficient' is a business-tunable policy tied to customer satisfaction targets and support cost goals.","testable_assertion":"Given a customer viewing the tracking page during active delivery, the displayed information includes at minimum an ETA and a delivery status."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in 'active' status","when":"The customer opens the tracking view","then":"The system displays delivery progress information (status, ETA, or abstracted proximity) without exposing the courier's exact GPS coordinates."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A delivery is in 'active' status","when":"The system receives a courier GPS update","then":"The raw coordinates are stored internally for operations but are never transmitted to any customer-facing API or UI."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A delivery transitions to 'completed' or 'cancelled'","when":"The customer views the tracking page","then":"No courier location or proximity data is displayed; only final delivery status is shown."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer is tracking an active delivery","when":"The courier is within a configurable proximity threshold of the delivery address","then":"The customer receives a notification such as 'Your courier is nearby' without revealing the exact position."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier GPS signal is lost during active delivery","expected_behavior":"The tracking view shows last known status and ETA with a stale-data indicator; it must not fall back to showing cached raw coordinates."},{"scenario":"Customer has multiple active deliveries from different couriers","expected_behavior":"Each delivery shows independent, isolated tracking progress; no courier's information leaks across orders."},{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., traffic, break)","expected_behavior":"ETA recalculates and status may update to 'delayed'; the system does not expose that the courier is stationary at a specific location."},{"scenario":"Customer attempts to access tracking via API directly","expected_behavior":"The API returns only abstracted progress data (status, ETA, proximity tier), never raw coordinates, regardless of how the request is constructed."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Getting this wrong in either direction has direct financial consequences: losing customers due to poor transparency (Customer Success concern) or losing couriers due to surveillance perception (Operations concern). Driver acquisition costs in logistics typically run $3,000-$5,000 per driver; customer acquisition cost varies but churn from poor tracking is measurable. A balanced solution protects both revenue streams.","operational":"Operations must still receive exact GPS data for dispatch optimization, route planning, and SLA monitoring. The constraint is on the customer-facing layer only. This means two views of the same data: an internal operational view (full fidelity) and an external customer view (abstracted).","user":"Customers get meaningful delivery progress (ETA, status, proximity) without the ethical and safety downsides of exact tracking. Couriers are protected from feeling surveilled, which directly impacts driver satisfaction and retention.","risk":"High risk if unresolved: implementing exact GPS per Customer Success will accelerate driver attrition; implementing zero visibility per Operations will increase support load and customer churn. There is also regulatory risk — GDPR and similar frameworks may restrict sharing worker location data with third parties without explicit consent.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive delivery progress transparency through abstracted means (ETA, status, proximity zones) rather than raw GPS. Slightly less granular but addresses the core need of knowing when their delivery arrives."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Protected from customer surveillance. Location data used internally only. Directly addresses retention concern raised by Operations."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Must accept that 'exact GPS coordinates at all times' is not viable. The transparency differentiator can be achieved through best-in-class ETA accuracy and proactive status updates instead."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Requirement to never show any location information is too absolute. Abstracted proximity (e.g., 'nearby', 'on the way') is not the same as showing GPS. Operations must accept a middle ground."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Sharing exact courier GPS with customers may violate worker privacy regulations in multiple jurisdictions. An abstracted approach reduces legal exposure."},{"group":"Support team","impact":"Better tracking reduces inbound 'where is my order' volume, which is a major cost center in courier operations."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The specification contains a direct, explicit contradiction between two stakeholder teams, both claiming non-negotiable positions. No product owner or executive arbiter has provided a resolution. While industry best practices clearly point toward abstracted tracking (as implemented by major players like UberEats, DoorDash, Amazon), the actual decision authority within this organization has not spoken. Confidence is low because no authoritative resolution has been provided.","high_confidence_areas":["Raw GPS coordinates should never be exposed to customers (industry standard, privacy law, driver safety)","Some form of delivery tracking must exist to remain competitive","Internal operations needs full GPS fidelity for dispatch and routing","Abstracted proximity and ETA-based tracking is the proven industry compromise"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which executive or product owner has final authority to resolve this conflict","Whether the organization has specific regulatory obligations around worker location data","The exact abstraction level acceptable to Customer Success (proximity zones vs. map animation vs. ETA only)","Whether couriers have contractual terms regarding location data sharing"],"assumptions":["No executive or product owner has yet arbitrated this conflict","Industry-standard abstracted tracking (ETA + proximity tiers) would satisfy the legitimate business need behind both teams' positions","The organization is subject to standard worker privacy considerations"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers as a non-negotiable transparency feature. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers as a non-negotiable driver retention measure. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as stated — the system cannot simultaneously show and not show courier location. Resolution requires an executive arbiter to set policy. The industry-standard resolution is abstracted tracking (ETA, status updates, proximity tiers) which satisfies Customer Success's transparency goal without creating the surveillance experience Operations objects to.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success team","Operations team","Couriers","Customers","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["Accept Customer Success position: show exact GPS (risks driver attrition and potential privacy violations)","Accept Operations position: show no location data (risks customer churn and increased support load)","Compromise via abstracted tracking: show ETA, delivery status, and proximity tiers without raw coordinates (industry standard approach used by Uber, Amazon, DoorDash)"],"recommendation":"Option 3 — abstracted tracking. This is the proven industry approach that addresses the legitimate business need behind both positions. Customer Success gets a transparency differentiator through best-in-class ETA and proactive notifications. Operations protects drivers from surveillance. An executive sponsor must formally approve this compromise and both teams must accept it as policy."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers","business_rule_broken":"BR-2 — Courier personally identifiable location data must not be exposed in a way that compromises driver safety or creates a surveillance experience"},{"violation":"Showing zero delivery progress information to customers","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 — During an active delivery, the customer must receive some form of delivery progress information"}]}
